sourceMovie.title: Lucy
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Lucy for the same reason it broke through in 2014: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a science fiction rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Luc Besson keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Matrix Resurrections
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Lucy clicked for you, The Matrix Resurrections (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Lucy?
faq.answer: Try The Matrix Resurrections, Bloodshot, Upgrade—each shares DNA with Lucy while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Lucy?
faq.answer: Lucy (2014) is best described as Action, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Lucy worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
